case.question: Which of the four labeled patterns exactly matches the continuous path shown in the TARGET box?
case.rubric: Option B perfectly replicates the target path tracing from the top-left dot, to the center, to the bottom-right, and finally to the bottom-center dot, whereas the other options deviate by a single dot.

planner_focus.weakness: finding the only exact match among near duplicates with tiny differences

case.prompt: Create one clean visual reasoning worksheet puzzle for early visual cognition skills. Use a clean printed worksheet style on a white background, crisp geometric shapes, small labels, no photorealism, no watermark, and no explanatory answer key. It must be unambiguous and solvable from the image alone. Do not include the written question in the image. A clean visual reasoning worksheet on a white background. Crisp geometric marks, no photorealism. Top center: A box labeled 'TARGET' containing a standard 3x3 grid of small black dots. A thick, crisp blue line traces a path connecting these dots: it starts at the Top-Left dot, goes straight to the Center dot, then to the Bottom-Right dot, and finally to the Bottom-Center dot. Below this target box, there are four more 3x3 grids of dots arranged in a horizontal row, clearly labeled 'A', 'B', 'C', and 'D'. Option A shows a path from Top-Left to Center to Bottom-Right to Bottom-Left. Option B is an exact match to the TARGET (Top-Left -> Center -> Bottom-Right -> Bottom-Center). Option C shows a path from Top-Center to Center to Bottom-Right to Bottom-Center. Option D shows a path from Top-Left to Center to Middle-Right to Bottom-Center. Do not include the puzzle question in the image, no watermarks, and no explanatory answer key.
